options.description: Copy the password to the clipboard
Display a QRcode of the password

states.name: pass-name
states.description: The password you want to show

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize new password storage and use gpg-id for encryption

options.description: A specific gpg-id or set of gpg-ids is assigned for that specific sub folder of the password store

states.name: gpg-id
states.description: The gpg-id you want to use to encrypt your password store

subcommands.name: insert
subcommands.description: Insert a new password into the password store called pass-name

options.description: Disable keyboard echo when the password is entered and confirm the password by asking for it twice
Lines will be read until EOF or Ctrl+D is reached. Otherwise, only a single line from standard in is read
Don't prompt before overwriting an existing password

subcommands.name: cp
subcommands.description: Copies the password or directory named old-path to new-path

options.description: Do not interactively prompt before moving

states.name: old-path
states.description: The old password name or directory

states.name: new-path
states.description: The new password name or directory

subcommands.name: mv
subcommands.description: Renames the password or directory named old-path to new-path

options.description: Do not interactively prompt before moving

states.name: old-path
states.description: The old password name or directory

states.name: new-path
states.description: The new password name or directory

subcommands.name: rm
subcommands.description: Remove the password named pass-name from the password store

options.description: Delete pass-name recursively if it is a directory
Do not interactively prompt before removal

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: Generate a new password of length pass-length and insert into pass-name

options.description: Do not use any non-alphanumeric characters in the generated password
Do not print the password but instead copy it to the clipboard
Do not interactively prompt, and only replace the first line of the password file with the new generated password, keeping the remainder of the file intact

subcommands.name: ls
subcommands.description: List names of passwords inside the tree at subfolder by using the tree

states.name: password-sub-directory
states.description: The password sub directory you want to list

subcommands.name: find
subcommands.description: List names of passwords inside the tree that match pass-names

states.name: pass-name
states.description: The password name you want to search for

options.description: Copy the password to the clipboard
Display a QRcode of the password

states.name: pass-name
states.description: The password you want to show

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: Insert a new password or edit an existing password using the default text editor specified by the environment

states.name: pass-name
states.description: The password you want to edit

subcommands.name: grep
subcommands.description: Searches inside each decrypted password file for search-string. Grep options can be used
